field_conventions.analyzer_node_type: lowercase_underscore identifier from CBM analyzer output
field_conventions.dr_layer: canonical DR layer name (one of 12 layers)
field_conventions.dr_node_type: node type within the DR layer
field_conventions.conditions.verify_role: optional role designation for verification scope (primary, secondary, auxiliary) — nested inside conditions object
field_conventions.conditions.evidence_fields: array of fields from analyzer output used as evidence for mapping confidence — nested inside conditions object
field_conventions.conditions.promotion_heuristics: conditions and thresholds for promoting nodes to higher-level element types — nested inside conditions object

mappings.description: HTTP route maps to API operation with high confidence
Function maps to application function; may be promoted to service via heuristics
Class method maps to application function; may be promoted to service via heuristics
Module (file/namespace) maps to application component with low confidence

unmapped_labels.reason: Project-level artifacts represent codebase metadata, not architecture model elements. Project identification is handled separately via project_identification in analyzer.json.
Package-level constructs map to technology.stack or technology.platform concepts, not discrete architecture elements. Requires semantic analysis to determine architectural significance.
Directory/folder structures are implementation details of code organization, not first-class architecture model elements. Mapping would create noise unless explicitly promoted via heuristics.
Individual files are too granular for architecture models. File-level structure is captured through source_reference provenance linking, not as elements.
Interface definitions are abstraction mechanisms within code (application layer), but not distinct element types in the DR model. Represented implicitly through applicationcomponent relationships.
Enumeration types represent constrained value sets, more suitable for data-model layer attributes than standalone elements. Captured through attribute definitions, not as first-class elements.
Type definitions (aliases, generics, unions) are type-level metadata without semantic architectural significance. Type information flows through attributes and element properties.
